summary:
Professional misconduct cases require patients' records to be promptly accessed; however, specific regulatory deadlines are not explicitly stated among current guidelines or laws referenced herein.
To improve accuracy by focusing more closely on existing standards without making assumptions beyond what is given: 

Summary: There isn't a clearly defined deadline from our sources regarding when patient records need to be supplied after reports of professional misconduct. Typically though, health care institutions aim to provide them quickly as part of their duty during investigations into alleged malpractice.
